title: Judicial activism in the Philippines
sub_topics: Article VIII Section 1 of the 1987 Constitution
ordinary vs expanded power of judicial review
libertarian jurisprudence of US Supreme Court
separate is not equal / read him his rights / one-man-one-vote / fruit of the poisonous tree

stances.claim: Article VIII Section 1 has two parts: ordinary judicial review (settling actual controversies) and the extraordinary certiorari power to determine grave abuse of discretion — by imposing the second as a duty rather than a mere power, the Constitution mandates judges to be activists.
Power can be relinquished, but a duty cannot under any circumstance be evaded — Philippine judges are denied the 'luxury' that American magistrates enjoy of choosing between passivism and activism.

The judiciary may not interfere with the INTERNAL rules of Congress or the INTERNAL orders of the President — but where the Constitution imposes conditions, limitations, or restrictions on the exercise of power, the judiciary may review compliance with those constraints.
stances.rhetorical_move: internal-vs-external boundary doctrine for separation of powers

stances.claim: Philippine judicial libertarianism is more secure than American because it is anchored on 'the impregnable Constitution' rather than on jurisprudence that can be changed anytime.
stances.rhetorical_move: comparative-constitutional argument for Philippine institutional advantage

institutions: Regional Trial Court of Naga City
Far Eastern University Central Student Organization
National Union of Students of the Philippines
events: Second impeachment complaint against CJ Davide (2003)

signature_phrases: Power can be relinquished, but a duty cannot under any circumstance be evaded
the luxury is denied to all Philippine judges
register_markers: THAT THE JUDICIARY MUST NOT ONLY BE
self-citation by exact dates of prior columns
decision_framework_signals: applies duty-vs-power doctrine to derive a constitutional mandate for activism
frames internal/external acts as the operative line for judicial intervention in legislative/executive procedure
uses comparative-constitutional reasoning (US vs PH) to show structural superiority of Philippine activism mandate
cross_references: The stirring American debate on the rightful role of the judiciary I discussed last Monday
I have written about him at least three times (Jan. 21, 2018, Oct. 11, 2020, and Nov. 8, 2020)
notable_anecdotes: Being the youngest president of FEU Central Student Organization and cofounder/president of the National Union of Students — finding Judge Santos a 'soulmate' on activism
